- **Reclaim `-e`/`--env` for runtime env vars** on `rise deploy` and
`rise run`, renaming build-time flags to `-b`/`--build-arg`

alias

| Command | Before | After |
|---------|--------|-------|

`--protected-env`, `--env-file` |

commit.message:
Adopt Change Proof PR descriptions (#36) closes #34 ### Behavior Delta | | Before | After | |---|---|---| | Trigger | PR authors filled a legacy `Context / TL;DR / Summary / Alternatives / Test Plan` template manually. | Agents now draft a reviewer-oriented Change Proof body and append an Architecture Trace section. | | Observable effect | Reviewers had structure, but not an explicit approval argument tied to invariants, evidence, blast radius, and rollback. | Reviewers get the behavior delta, invariants, validation evidence, risk/rollback, review focus, and a C4 appendix in one validated shape. | | Affected inputs | Any PR description checked by `mix pr_body.check` and the `validate-pr-description` workflow. | The repository PR template, the local validator, and the agent skill instructions used during PR creation. | ```mermaid flowchart TD Issue[Issue context + diff] --> Skill[pr-description skill] Skill --> Template[Change Proof + Architecture Trace body] Template --> Validator[mix pr_body.check] Validator --> Reviewer[Reviewer confidence gate] ``` ### Invariants / Non-goals - **Must still hold**: PR descriptions stay GitHub-native markdown, can still be checked with `mix pr_body.check`, and still support pure-refactor PRs. - **Explicitly unchanged**: The PR lint workflow name/context (`validate-pr-description`) and the underlying GitHub PR flow remain in place. - **Out of scope**: Automatically collecting artifact links or generating permanent code links from tooling at runtime. ### Validation | Risk point | Evidence | Link | |---|---|---| | New required sections are not enforced | `mix test test/mix/tasks/pr_body_check_test.exs` | N/A (local command) | | Pure-refactor / no-decision appendix path regresses | `mix test test/mix/tasks/pr_body_check_test.exs` | N/A (local command) | | A real draft body does not satisfy the validator | `mix pr_body.check --file /tmp/gh34_pr_body.md` | N/A (local command) | | Broader repo checks regress | `make all` | N/A (local command) | ### Risk / Blast Radius / Rollback - **Most likely failure**: An authored PR body looks structurally correct but omits the required approval evidence or appendix semantics. - **Blast radius**: Repository PR authoring guidance, the local PR-body mix task, the PR description GitHub workflow, and agent skill instructions for Codex/Claude. - **How to detect**: `mix pr_body.check` fails locally, `validate-pr-description` fails in CI, or reviewers still cannot answer the 6 verification questions from the body. - **How to rollback**: Revert the template, skill, and validator changes together. ### Review Focus 1. Check that the repository template and both agent skills now encode the same Change Proof + Architecture Trace structure. 2. Confirm `Mix.Tasks.PrBody.Check` enforces the approval-oriented sections, appendix wrapper, numbered review focus, and decision-record rules instead of only mirroring arbitrary headings. 3. Verify the validator still accepts the pure-refactor / `No design decision introduced in this PR.` path.
Architecture Trace ### Context (C4-L1) No L1 change - this PR only changes repository automation and reviewer guidance; it does not alter external system interactions. ### Container (C4-L2) Single-container change within the repository/tooling path. The affected runtime container is the Elixir automation service that validates PR descriptions. ### Component (C4-L3) The `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` file defines the reviewer-facing contract, `Mix.Tasks.PrBody.Check` enforces it locally and in CI, and the Codex/Claude `pr-description` skills tell agents how to draft bodies that satisfy the contract. ```mermaid flowchart LR Codex[Codex push/pr-description skills] --> Body[PR body draft] Claude[Claude gh-issue-exec/pr-description skills] --> Body Body --> Template[.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md] Template --> Check[Mix.Tasks.PrBody.Check] Check --> CI[pr-description-lint workflow] ``` ### Code Trace (C4-L4) - Template contract -> `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` -> `/.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` - Validator -> `Mix.Tasks.PrBody.Check` -> `/elixir/lib/mix/tasks/pr_body.check.ex` - Validator coverage -> `Mix.Tasks.PrBody.CheckTest` -> `/elixir/test/mix/tasks/pr_body_check_test.exs` - Codex authoring flow -> `push` + `pr-description` skills -> `/.codex/skills/push/SKILL.md`, `/.codex/skills/pr-description/SKILL.md` - Claude authoring flow -> `gh-issue-exec` + `pr-description` skills -> `/.claude/skills/gh-issue-exec/SKILL.md`, `/.claude/skills/pr-description/SKILL.md` ### Decision Record - **Decision**: Enforce the new reviewer-confidence PR structure directly in `mix pr_body.check` while also adding explicit authoring skills for Codex and Claude. - **Alternatives considered**: Keep a generic heading-mirror validator and rely on template comments alone; update only the markdown template without changing agent workflows. - **Trade-offs**: The validator becomes more opinionated and test-heavy, but CI now verifies the sections the ticket actually cares about. - **Why chosen**: The issue requires the approval-oriented sections, the always-present appendix, and the decision-record semantics to be enforced consistently across authoring and validation. - **Implementation links**: `/.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`, `/elixir/lib/mix/tasks/pr_body.check.ex`, `/elixir/test/mix/tasks/pr_body_check_test.exs`, `/.codex/skills/pr-description/SKILL.md`, `/.claude/skills/pr-description/SKILL.md`
--------- Co-authored-by: Codex Co-authored-by: Codex

commit.message:
feat(observability): expose session stats and completed runs (#37)

#### Context

The previous PR was already merged, while this branch adds new
observability work around session history, token cost estimates, and
review handoff cleanup.

#### TL;DR

*Track recent completed runs and expose session stats, model info, and
cost estimates in the observability APIs and dashboard.*

#### Summary

- Persist completed session records in the workspace and load them into
orchestrator snapshots
- Expose runtime version, configured model, completed runs, and cost
estimates in presenter payloads
- Add SessionStats unit tests and update observability/orchestrator
expectations for the richer payloads
- Remove the explicit `@codex` review request step from
`elixir/WORKFLOW.md` handoff guidance

#### Alternatives

- Keep the data only in logs, but that makes dashboards and API
consumers reconstruct session history themselves
- Expose only running-session metadata, but that leaves no lightweight
way to inspect recent completed runs

#### Test Plan

- [x] `make -C elixir all`
- [x] `cd elixir && mise exec -- mix test
test/symphony_elixir/extensions_test.exs
test/symphony_elixir/orchestrator_status_test.exs
test/symphony_elixir/session_stats_test.exs`

---------

commit.message:
fix(github): improve auth fallback handling (#35)

#### Context

Improve GitHub auth fallback behavior in the container runtime and align
the workflow guide with token-based GitHub access.

#### TL;DR

*Persist `gh` auth from token env vars and relax workflow blocker rules
around GitHub access.*

#### Summary

- Log `gh` into github.com from `GH_TOKEN` or `GITHUB_TOKEN` when
available

Adds 13 new scenarios targeting previously uncovered decision_api.lua branches:
- rule_engine returns nil/invalid decision → 503

- JWT with 4+ segments (too many dots) → nil
- JWT with 1 segment (only one dot) → nil
- ip_country falls back to X-Country-Code header when geoip var absent
- empty host variable → nil host in request context
- allow decision with no headers proceeds without exiting

commit.message:
fix(safety): add approval guardrails and safer fetches (#29)

* docs(design): add phase 3 TUI refinement design doc

Add design document for Phase 3 TUI refinement, focusing on:
- Content-first minimalist design philosophy
- Removal of Panel borders and visual clutter
- Refined color palette inspired by Claude Code
- Component specifications with before/after comparisons
- Migration plan from Phase 2 to cleaner terminal UI

* feat(tui): implement Phase 3 TUI refinement

Redesign the terminal UI to be more elegant and content-first:

- Replace Panel borders with plain text layout
- Add Theme class with refined color palette (DIM, SUCCESS, ERROR)
- Add STATUS_ICONS with spinner (⠋), checkmark (✓), and error (✗) indicators
- Simplify session header to single line with path truncation
- Refactor help display to use aligned columns with partition()
- Refactor status display to use batched console output
- Refactor history display with turn separators and limit parameter
- Update prompt symbol to amber-colored ›
- Remove unused rich imports (Panel, Rule, Table)
- Add docstrings to all public methods

Fixes from simplify review:
- Replace cryptic 'w' variable with 'label_width'
- Cache excess count to avoid double len() check in show_history()

All 66 tests pass.

* fix(tui): address Phase 3 TUI refinement review comments

- Wire custom amber › prompt indicator into REPL by making prompt_text
  optional in PromptInput protocol and calling prompt() without args
- Re-truncate turn text in /history output to 80 chars to keep it compact
- Remove duplicated newline hint from /help (already in command_help_lines)

* fix(cli): close TUI review regressions

Summary:
- align help rendering for all slash commands, including /history
- update the smoke prompt double to match the optional REPL prompt
  signature
- add a presenter regression test for consistent help formatting

Rationale:
- the TUI refinement left one malformed help row because rendering
  depended on spacing in the source strings
- the REPL prompt fix changed the protocol shape and broke the
  interactive smoke path until the fake was updated to match

Tests:
- PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 uv run pytest -p no:cacheprovider
  tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py
  tests/smoke/test_cli_chat.py::test_run_chat_command_interactive_starts_fresh_session_each_time

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Increase default max_iterations from 8 to 80

* docs(design): align phase-3 tool policy docs with actual implementation

Update the design doc to reflect the shipped behavior where all tools
are enabled by default, with --deny-* flags to disable them:

- ToolPolicy defaults: allow_write=True, allow_bash=True (was False)
- CLI flags: --deny-write, --deny-bash, --deny-network (was --allow-*)
- Add policy resolution order documentation
- Update Write, Edit, Bash tool sections to reflect enable-by-default

The docs now match the actual code in policy.py, main.py, and tests.

* fix(cli): show enabled tools in status

Summary:
- thread enabled tool names from the tool registry into /status
- render a Tools line in the presenter and document the behavior
- add a CLI regression test for interactive status output

Rationale:
- Phase 3 docs call out the enabled tool set as recommended startup
- and status behavior, but the REPL only surfaced session metadata.
- Exposing registry-backed tool names keeps the CLI aligned with runtime
- policy without duplicating tool configuration state.

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py
- broader run of tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py and
  tests/smoke/test_cli_chat.py hit an unrelated existing failure in
  tests/smoke/test_cli_chat.py:170

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(tools): harden default tool output

Summary:
- preserve structured tool summaries in the TUI and keep long token
  arguments identifiable
- make web_fetch byte-limited decoding UTF-8 safe and normalize ddgs
  source metadata
- expand filesystem and tool registry coverage and align phase-3 docs
  with the shipped behavior

Rationale:
- tool activity lines need readable identifiers for paths and URLs
- byte-aware decoding and non-ASCII result content avoid data loss at
  tool boundaries
- docs and tests should reflect the actual default tool behavior

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py
  tests/unit/test_local_fs_tools.py tests/unit/test_web_tools.py
  tests/unit/test_tool_registry.py tests/smoke/test_cli_chat.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(safety): add approval guardrails and safer fetches

Summary:
- restore the default agent loop safety budget to 8
- add CLI and settings overrides for max_iterations and
  max_html_extract_bytes
- persist oversized web_fetch results as artifacts and fail safely when
  truncated HTML extraction cannot produce readable text
- require approval for write/edit, bash, and network tools with a session
  auto-approve path

Rationale:
- keep unsafe autonomy bounded until runtime approval guardrails exist
- borrow mature coding-agent patterns without copying their larger default
  loop budgets blindly
- avoid returning corrupted fetch output when large HTML pages are truncated

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_approvals.py
  tests/unit/test_tool_registry.py tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py
  tests/smoke/test_cli_chat.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(cli): validate max settings and prompt only on tty

Summary:
- reject non-positive max iteration and HTML extract byte values at
  the CLI boundary before persisting or running chat
- require both stdin and stdout to be TTYs before prompting for
  unsafe tool approvals in one-shot mode
- add regression tests for invalid init values and redirected stdout

Rationale:
- prevent `agentlet init` from writing settings that later fail to
  load during normal chat usage
- avoid leaking approval prompts into redirected output and hanging
  scripts that expect one-shot stdout capture

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_settings.py
  tests/unit/test_cli_approvals.py tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback for safety guardrails

- Fix truncated User-Agent string in web_fetch tool
- Add 10MB maximum bound for max_html_extract_bytes setting
- Document behavioral change: allow_write/allow_bash default to True
- Set restrictive permissions (0o600) on fetch artifact files

* fix(cli): preserve approvals and history labels

Summary:
- route one-shot approval prompts through stderr so stdout stays clean
- carry original turn numbers through history summaries and rendering
- add regression tests for redirected approvals and trimmed history output

Rationale:
- one-shot approvals should still work when command output is redirected
- history labels must match the real transcript after trimming recent turns

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py tests/unit/test_cli_approvals.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(cli): address Phase 3 code review feedback for safety guardrails

Issue 1: Allow approval prompts when stdin is redirected
- Add /dev/tty fallback in InteractiveApprovalHandler for piped input
- Add _tty_attempted flag to cache failed open attempts
- Add context manager support and close() method for resource cleanup
- Add isatty() validation on fallback TTY

Issue 2: Enforce max_html_extract_bytes upper bound in CLI
- Add MAX_HTML_EXTRACT_BYTES_LIMIT constant to policy.py
- Consolidate _int_at_least and _int_in_range into single function
- Apply range validation to both init and chat subcommands
- Update settings.py to use the shared constant

All 107 tests pass.

* fix(cli): convert REPL to async for proper approval handling

Convert run_repl() to async to fix asyncio.run() nesting error when
tool approval prompts are triggered during interactive sessions.

- Change run_repl() to async def and use await for loop.run_turn()
- Move asyncio.run() to entry point in chat_app.py
- Remove unused asyncio import from repl.py

This fixes the "asyncio.run() cannot be called from a running event loop"
error and ensures approval prompts are routed to the correct handler
instead of being captured as new REPL queries.

* fix(cli): use prompt_async for async REPL

Add prompt_async method to PromptInput protocol and use it in run_repl()
to properly support async execution with prompt_toolkit.

- Add prompt_async to PromptInput protocol in repl.py
- Use await prompt_input.prompt_async() instead of prompt()
- Update FakePromptSession in tests to implement prompt_async

This fixes the event loop conflict when running REPL in async mode.

* fix(cli): harden interactive approval prompts

Summary:
- use async approval prompts in the interactive REPL to avoid event-loop
  reentrancy failures
- close approval handlers in one-shot and interactive chat flows to
  release any fallback tty handles
- narrow approval-prompt EOF handling with a dedicated exception and add
  CLI regression coverage for approval, EOF, and provider failure paths

Rationale:
- prompt_toolkit approvals run inside an active event loop, so reusing the
  sync prompt path caused asyncio.run() failures during interactive turns
- approval prompt closure should end the session cleanly without masking
  unrelated EOF errors from providers or tools
- explicit lifecycle cleanup and focused regression tests keep the CLI
  approval flow stable as the TUI evolves

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_approvals.py tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py tests/smoke/test_cli_chat.py -q

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(agent-loop): stop re-running stale approved tool calls

Summary:
- ignore residual tool_calls on terminal assistant responses
- keep final assistant messages and session records free of stale calls
- add regression coverage for agent loop and interactive approval flow

Rationale:
- some streamed provider responses can end with finish_reason=stop
  while still carrying old tool_calls in the payload
- treating those stale calls as executable caused repeated approval
  prompts after the final answer and could loop indefinitely

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_agent_loop.py
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py -k "interactive_approval
  or stale_tool_calls or one_shot_approval_prompts"
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_approvals.py
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_litellm_provider.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* test(cli): harden interactive chat coverage

Summary:
- add direct run_repl tests for commands, interrupts, cancellation,
  and failure recovery
- add prompt_toolkit smoke coverage for multiline terminal input and
  interactive approval flow
- keep transcript assertions in the smoke path so interactive state is
  validated end to end

Rationale:
- interactive bugs were clustering around branches that fake provider
  tests hit only indirectly
- covering both REPL control flow and real prompt_toolkit input makes
  regressions in terminal-mode chat easier to catch early

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_repl.py
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py tests/unit/test_cli_approvals.py
  tests/smoke/test_cli_chat.py tests/unit/test_sessions.py

* fix(cli): restore repl prompt after approval

Summary:
- restore the main PromptSession message after interactive approval prompts
- add regression tests for sticky approval prompts in the handler and chat flow

Rationale:
- prevent approval prompt text from persisting into the main REPL input
- stop yes/all replies from being misrouted as normal user messages

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_approvals.py tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py -q
- uv run pytest tests/smoke/test_cli_chat.py -q
